source: Ahmed, Tarek: Reservoir Engineering Handbook, 4th ed. (Elsevier, 2010), Chapter 10 'Water Influx', Example 10-10, pp. 726-729. The example's footnote credits the data to L.P. Dake, Fundamentals of Reservoir Engineering (Elsevier, 1978) - the same wedge reservoir-aquifer system as Dake Exercise 9.2 (validation CASE 2C), here worked with Fetkovich's method and a printed step-by-step We table. Typed verbatim from the book PDF (irmat-ucan.com library copy) on 2026-07-18.
notes: The printed solution shows the p_r-bar convention explicitly: (p_r-bar)_n = (p_(n-1) + p_n)/2, the step midpoint of reservoir pressures, driving dp_n = (p_a-bar)_(n-1) - (p_r-bar)_n. This ARBITRATES the Fetkovich dp-bar convention question paused in ReservoirBalance-STATUS: the engine's midpoint convention is the published one.

Book J formula for the radial aquifer with a no-flow outer boundary carries the -3/4 term: J = 0.00708 k h f / (mu_w (ln reD - 0.75)); the printed 116.5 bbl/day/psi is reproduced only with the -0.75 included (the PDF's equation rendering drops it, the arithmetic confirms it).
Printed intermediate constants: Wei = 211.9 MM bbl, J pi/Wei = 1.506e-3, decay term (1 - exp(-J pi dt/Wei)) = 0.4229 for dt = 365 days, reduced marching constant Wei/pi * 0.4229 = 32,705 bbl/psi.
Book rounds the average aquifer pressures to whole psi (2689, 2565, 2409) and dWe to 3-4 significant figures; per-step tolerances in CASE 8 allow for that rounding.
